message:
feat(agentmail): add direct GetMessage fetch with inbox-scan fallback

Three changes to improve Agent Mail reliability and performance:

1. Direct message retrieval:
   UnifiedMessenger.Read() now calls GetMessage() to fetch a single
   message by ID instead of scanning the entire inbox. This is O(1)
   instead of O(n) where n is the inbox size, which matters for agents
   with hundreds of messages. If GetMessage is unavailable (older
   Agent Mail servers), it falls back to the previous inbox-scanning
   approach with a debug log, maintaining backward compatibility.

2. EnsureProject retry logic:
   EnsureProject() now uses callToolWithBusyRetry() (3 retries, 3s
   backoff) instead of a single callTool(). This is the first call
   made when multiple agents start simultaneously against the same
   project, and the Agent Mail server can return transient busy errors
   during concurrent project creation. Without retry, agents would
   fail to initialize their mail subsystem.

3. Test updates:
   - fakeAMClient extended with GetMessage() support and tracking
   - TestUnifiedMessengerRead_AgentMailMarksRead verifies GetMessage
     is called before MarkMessageRead
   - TestUnifiedMessengerRead_AgentMailFetchesDeeperHistory forces
     GetMessage to fail to exercise the fallback inbox-scan path

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
